content.scenario: Incident Response Basics 🛡️: NIST IR Lifecycle
content.text: The four main phases of the NIST Incident Response lifecycle are Preparation, Detection & Analysis, {{c1::Containment, Eradication & Recovery}}, and Post-Incident Activity.
content.explanation: This cyclical process provides an industry-standard framework. Learning from the incident (post-incident activity) directly feeds back into improving the preparation phase.
content.spanish: Las cuatro fases principales del ciclo de vida de Respuesta a Incidentes de NIST son Preparación, Detección y Análisis, Contención, Erradicación y Recuperación, y Actividad Post-Incidente.
content.usage:
NIST IR Lifecycle Flow:
graph TD Prep["1. Preparation"] Detect["2. Detection & Analysis"] Contain["3. Containment, Eradication & Recovery"] Post["4. Post-Incident Activity"] Prep --> Detect Detect --> Contain Contain --> Post Post --> Prep end

content.text: An artifact indicating that a system has already been breached (e.g. file hashes, IP addresses) is called an {{c1::Indicator of Compromise (IoC)}}.
content.explanation: IoCs are reactive evidence (like a known bad IP in connection logs) showing that an exploit was successful. In contrast, an Indicator of Attack (IoA) focuses on real-time behavior and active tactics.
content.spanish: Un artefacto que indica que un sistema ya ha sido vulnerado (como hashes de archivos, direcciones IP) se denomina Indicador de Compromiso (IoC).
content.usage: Examples of IoCs: MD5 file hashes, malicious domains, modified registry paths

content.scenario: Incident Response Basics 🛡️: Forensics Volatility Framework
content.text: The leading open-source command-line framework used by forensic analysts to analyze volatile RAM memory dumps is {{c1::Volatility}}.
content.explanation: Allows analysts to reconstruct the state of a system at the time of the RAM capture, extracting running processes, active network connections, kernel modules, and cached registry keys
content.spanish: El framework de línea de comandos de código abierto líder utilizado por los analistas forenses para analizar volcados de memoria RAM volátil es Volatility.
content.usage: Typical workflow: Capture RAM (using FTK Imager) → Analyze with Volatility CLI

content.scenario: Incident Response Operations ⚙️: Volatility Process Enumeration
content.text: In the Volatility 3 framework, to extract and list the running processes from a Windows memory dump, you run the plugin {{c1::`windows.pslist.PsList`}}.
content.explanation: This plugin traverses the active process list linked-list structure in kernel memory (ActiveProcessLinks) to enumerate all processes that were active when memory was dumped.
content.spanish: En el framework Volatility 3, para extraer y listar los procesos en ejecución desde un volcado de memoria de Windows, ejecutas el complemento windows.pslist.PsList.

content.scenario: Incident Response Operations ⚙️: Containment Phase Objectives
content.text: During an active malware outbreak, the primary objective of the containment phase is to {{c1::limit the scope of the incident}} to prevent further lateral spread.
content.explanation: Containment must occur before eradication. Isolating the affected machines (e.g. disabling switch ports or applying network firewall blocks) prevents the threat from moving to other parts of the network.
content.spanish: Durante un brote de malware activo, el objetivo principal de la fase de contención es limitar el alcance del incidente para evitar una mayor propagación lateral.
content.usage: Actions: Host isolation, shutting down network ports, suspending user accounts

content.scenario: Incident Response Operations ⚙️: Volatile Memory Priority
content.text: According to the Order of Volatility, forensic analysts must capture {{c1::CPU registers and RAM}} first, as this data is lost immediately when the host machine is powered off.
content.explanation: Disk forensics targets non-volatile data. Memory forensics targets volatile data (like running malware processes, unencrypted keys, or network connections) which is completely wiped during reboot or power down.
content.spanish: De acuerdo con el Orden de Volatilidad, los analistas forenses deben capturar los registros de la CPU y la RAM primero, ya que estos datos se pierden inmediatamente cuando se apaga la máquina host.
content.usage: Order: 1. Registers/Cache, 2. Routing Table/RAM, 3. Temporary Files, 4. Hard Drive

content.scenario: Incident Response Engineering 🛠️: Forensic Command Line Extraction
content.text: In Volatility 3, to view the exact command-line arguments passed to running processes, you run the plugin {{c1::`windows.cmdline.CmdLine`}}.
content.explanation: Process lists can show legitimate names (like svchost.exe), but looking at the command line arguments reveals if the process was launched from a suspicious path or executed malicious arguments.
content.spanish: En Volatility 3, para ver los argumentos exactos de la línea de comandos pasados a los procesos en ejecución, ejecutas el complemento windows.cmdline.CmdLine.

content.text: During the eradication phase, rather than trying to delete individual malware files, the recommended best practice is to {{c1::rebuild the operating system from a trusted clean image}}.
content.explanation: Malware can leave registry backdoors or persistence files that manual cleanup might miss. Rebuilding a clean host guarantees all malicious code is eradicated completely.
content.spanish: Durante la fase de erradicación, en lugar de intentar eliminar archivos de malware individuales, la práctica recomendada es reconstruir el sistema operativo a partir de una imagen limpia y confiable.
content.usage: Eradication checklist: Rebuild OS from golden image → Restore data from clean backups → Patch host vulnerabilities

content.scenario: Incident Response Engineering 🛠️: Root Cause Analysis
content.text: To prevent similar security breaches in the future, responders perform a {{c1::Root Cause Analysis (RCA)}} to identify the fundamental vulnerability that allowed the compromise.
content.explanation: Simply restoring system services is not enough. RCA identifies *how* the attacker got in (e.g. unpatched exploit, spear phishing) so the security team can fix the underlying flaw.
content.spanish: Para prevenir violaciones de seguridad similares en el futuro, los equipos de respuesta realizan un Análisis de Causa Raíz (RCA) para identificar la vulnerabilidad fundamental que permitió el compromiso.
content.usage: Methods: The 5 Whys, Fishbone diagrams, Event timelines

content.scenario: Incident Response Engineering 🛠️: Forensic Volatility Network Analysis
content.text: In Volatility 3, to list active network connections and sockets from a Windows RAM dump, you use the plugin {{c1::`windows.netscan.NetScan`}}.
content.explanation: The NetScan plugin scans for TCP connections, UDP listeners, and raw sockets, allowing the analyst to identify outbound connections back to the attacker's Command and Control (C2) servers.
content.spanish: En Volatility 3, para listar sockets y conexiones de red activas desde un volcado de memoria RAM de Windows, utilizas el complemento windows.netscan.NetScan.
